methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Vannevar Labs, a Series B defense and security company founded by Brett Granberg and Nini Moorhead, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.5B following $90M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities operating in the same sector and stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage advancement, yielding $2.9B. Peer-Median analysis examines five same-sector, same-stage private comparables (Dream Security at $1.5B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, Castelion, Defense Unicorns, and Heven AeroTech each at $1.0B), producing a median of $1.0B and a range of $1.0B–$1.5B. The stage-progression method assumes normal capital markets progression; the peer-median method anchors to direct market precedent but reflects median rather than top-quartile positioning.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $850M to $3.3B, with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits at the lower-median end of this range. Key uncertainties include capital markets conditions affecting step-up assumptions and the degree to which Vannevar's product maturity and revenue profile align with the selected peer cohort's commercial stage.
